description: i would like to organize all helpe functions/ classes that a person would need for a custom controller into a specific folder organized. i would like to have a unified beharior class that is easily usable and easily accsersssible. i would like to make it clear what each custom controller default fiunctions need to be and how each of those needs a default function in the default functrion controller thats easy to call. unify all code a user would need for there own custom controllers as much as possible. make sure the base ustom controller a user creates via animation editor windowis fully flushed out and has all the needed things nd there is a matching implemetnation in the defaultcustomo conterler call

make the weighted random range slider more user  friendly. its a bit clunky moves too fast too slow looks weird. make it easier for peaople to use and understand. make it easy to get fine numbervalues but also somehow move it very large way shorterly (variable drag speed based of distance for hyper specific and hyper fast) be creative make it user friendly. maybe even add a button that when clicked will open a largeish popup fthat bigger for editing things more accuratly

for anchor point editong in the asset info ui editor stack: when adjusting the depth of an anchor point instead of incrementing / deincrementing by deciaml percentages increment and deincrement by 1 px each scroll tick.

for dynamic asset efficiency rendering, for any asset that is between the efficiency depth and the cull depth, suspend animation advancment (freeze them such that  we save some prossessing time). also for efficieny depoth logic, instead of thining out the number of assets as it approaches the horizon, replace it with a fog assets. make sure we arnt wasting time calcualting fog interpolations for assets above the efficiency depth line as they will be pasued and 100% oppacity. implement all of this cleanly foir dynamic rendering efficiency logic whereever relevant
move the "near fog" slider from the dynamic assets panel into the camera panel (dev mode editor (room editor))

Asset Info Stack > Movement Editor: split point interaction into two handles for movement keys with non-zero DY. In RoomEditor movement editing, selecting a point with vertical offset should not snap the full 3D point to floor-projected mouse coordinates. Instead, drag on the ground crosshair handle to edit only X/Z (DX/DZ), and drag the elevated point handle (or wheel Y workflow) to edit only Y (DY). Update hit-testing and drag routing in the RoomEditor movement interaction path and keep RoomMovementToolsPanel numeric fields synchronized. Constraints: preserve existing snap-to-grid behavior for X/Z when snapping is enabled, avoid regressions for DY==0 points, and ensure no unintended floor re-projection of Y-offset points.

Asset Info Stack > Movement Editor path loading bug: when switching animation and path selection, always bind the editor to the selected animation's actual movement_paths data instead of stale or zero-filled frames. Investigate movement path state handoff between animation selection and path UI (RoomEditor subview state + RoomMovementToolsPanel path list callbacks, plus movement payload parsing/building in FrameEditState helpers). Implementation direction: on animation change, rebuild path options from that animation payload, clamp selected_path_index, and refresh visible point/frame data from the newly selected path. Constraints/cleanup: eliminate state leakage from previously viewed animations/paths, keep path counts in sync with movement_paths JSON, and prevent fallback 0,0,0 rows unless the authored path is truly empty.

Asset Info Stack > Movement Editor Y-adjust workflow: fix wheel-based vertical editing so DY updates live and does not alter X/Z. In movement edit mode, mouse-wheel height adjustment should switch visualization from floor grid to XY grid, keep XY grid resolution aligned with floor-grid spacing, and persist XY view until a click exits back to floor grid. Runtime behavior: if snapping is ON, each wheel step snaps to the next resolved 3D Y level at current X/Z; if snapping is OFF, each wheel tick moves Y by exactly 1 pixel/unit. Relevant systems: RoomEditor movement input handling, movement point transform updates, and movement overlay/grid rendering. Constraints: scrolling must never shift horizontal position, DY changes must be immediately visible in viewport and in RoomMovementToolsPanel Y field, and grid mode transitions should be explicit and reversible.

TaskEditor overlay fixes (ENGINE/runtime/utils/utils/task_editor.cpp): (1) clicking Add Task must create exactly one new TaskRow per click (investigate button event edge handling so add_task() is not invoked twice for a single click lifecycle), (2) while any TaskEditor input is active, keyboard/text events must be captured exclusively by TaskEditor and not propagate to gameplay/editor controls in AssetsManager/room input flows, and (3) description DMTextBox must support mouse caret placement so users can click to reposition cursor inside existing text. Constraints/cleanup: preserve current CSV persistence format, keep per-row last_updated updates on real edits only, and avoid breaking dropdown/attachment/delete interactions in the same table row.
